Tests that `Math.max` returns `NaN` when called with an empty object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that checks the behavior of the `Math.max` function when it receives an object as an argument. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of `Math.max(Object())` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The script demonstrates a specific edge case where `Math.max` is given a non-numeric input, ensuring that the function correctly returns `NaN` in such scenarios. This test is useful for validating the robustness of the `Math.max` function in handling unexpected input types.
